rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN GATE RESTAURANTGROUP, INC., ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OFINDUSTRIAL RELATIONS, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate Or Other Appropriate Relief
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 24, 2025, line 1. PETITIONER GOLDEN GATE RESTAURANT GROUP, INC., MONU SINGH, HARKIRAN RANDHAWA'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate Or Other Appropriate Relief. Petitioner Golden Gate Restaurant Group's writ of administrative mandate is denied. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. JOHNREY I MAGCALING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Stipulated Judgment Under Terms Of Stipulated Settlement
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 24, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S Motion For Stipulated Judgment Under Terms Of Stipulated Settlement. The unopposed motion for stipulated judgment pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 664.6 is granted. Moving party shall submit a proposed judgment in favor of Capital One Bank (USA), N.A. against Johnrey I. Magcaling in the amount of $13,035.00 plus court costs of $517. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK VS. ADAM CHREKI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 24, 2025, line 5. DEFENDANT ADAM CHREKI'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant Adam Chreki's motion for a deemed-admitted order as to his first set of requests for admission is denied. Chreki filed his motion before the extension of time he gave to plaintiff American Express National Bank to respond to the RFAs lapsed. (Conley Decl., para. 8 & Ex. 5.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: HARMONY OSWALD VS. Y COMBINATOR MANAGEMENT, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 24, 2025, line 9. DEFENDANTS Y COMBINATOR MANAGEMENT, LLC, Y COMBINATOR ES20, LLC, Y COMBINATOR ES24, LLC, YC AFFILIATES FUND II, L.P., YC AFFILIATES FUND II(QP), L.P., YC AFFILIATES FUND IIA, L.P.'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.
